title: Configure an HPA with correct scaling thresholds
scenario: The `api` Deployment is live and the team wants autoscaling. Someone already created an HPA but set the wrong values: `minReplicas: 1` (too low — you always want at least 2 for HA) and `targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 50` (too aggressive — use 70% to avoid thrashing). Fix both values.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: api-hpa
  namespace: default
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: api
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 50

hints.text: Two numbers need to change: `minReplicas` and `averageUtilization`. `maxReplicas` and the target Deployment reference are already correct.
Change `minReplicas: 1` to `minReplicas: 2` for high-availability. Change `averageUtilization: 50` to `averageUtilization: 70` to reduce scale-out sensitivity.
After applying, check `kubectl get hpa api-hpa`. The TARGETS column will show current CPU% vs 70%. The MINPODS column will show 2.

solution:
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: api-hpa
  namespace: default
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: api
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
